title: rsa_get_size result validated >0 not just success rc
description: After wolfhsm_rsa_get_size returns a successful rc (0), the code additionally checks that out_size > 0 before casting to u32. A future contributor might argue the >0 check is redundant because a successful rc guarantees a valid size. It is not redundant: out_size is a c_int output parameter; the C API contract does not prevent a buggy or adversarial server from returning rc=0 with out_size=0 or a negative value. Casting a zero or negative c_int to u32 would produce a nonsensical key_size_bytes value that would later cause a zero-length or gigantic allocation in raw_op(). The >0 guard is a defensive boundary check at the FFI boundary, which is the right place for it per the codebase's safety conventions.
